article_title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.5, the First Fully Retrained Base Model Since GPT-4.5, With 1M-Token Context and State-of-the-Art Agentic Benchmarks

content_preview.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.5, the First Fully Retrained Base Model Since GPT-4.5, With 1M-Token Context and State-of-the-Art Agentic Benchmarks
content_preview.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 arrives as a ground-up retrain with a 922K-token context window, 82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0, and two-tier pricing starting at $5/$30 per million tokens.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenAI on April 23, 2026 released GPT-5.5, describing it as the first fully retrained base model since GPT-4.5 and a significant architectural departure from the post-training iterations that produced GPT-5.1 through GPT-5.4. The model rolls out immediately to ChatGPT Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise users, with API access to follow after additional safety review. A more capable GPT-5.5 Pro variant is available to Pro, Business, and Enterprise subscribers at substantially higher ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent, balanced AI model coverage with honest competitive context including where GPT-5.5 trails. Good use of multiple sources. Appropriate skepticism in 'What Remains Unclear' section.
editor_notes.source_verification: Fetched all 5 sources. TechCrunch confirms Brockman and Pachocki quotes (verbatim match), release date (April 23), availability tiers. SiliconAngle confirms FrontierMath Tier 4 (39.6% vs 22.9%), Ramsey numbers, 20% token generation speed increase. Fello AI confirms 922K context window, Terminal-Bench 82.7%, GDPval 84.9%, OSWorld 78.7%, SWE-Bench 58.6%, Artificial Analysis #2 out of 141, Mythos comparison ('6 of 9 overlapping' matches article's '6 overlapping benchmarks'), 'best model most people can actually use' quote. OFox confirms pricing ($5/$30 standard, $30/$180 Pro, 50% batch discount) and agentic workflow description. Interesting Engineering confirms Terminal-Bench 82.7% and 71,000+ pages tax form example.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Most claims confirmed. Two items not visible in WebFetch output: (1) 'NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 infrastructure...35 times lower cost' is not in the OFox source — source is attributed correctly but this specific hardware claim was not visible. (2) AA-Omniscience benchmark scores (GPT-5.5 86% vs Opus 4.7 36%) and multilingual QA scores (91.5% vs 83.2%) attributed to Fello AI were not visible in WebFetch summary — these may appear in the full article. The benchmark inversion note ('higher score = lower factual accuracy') for AA-Omniscience is an important editorial note that should be verified.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Commendably balanced — acknowledges where GPT-5.5 underperforms Claude Opus 4.7.
editor_notes.originality: No prior GPT-5.5 coverage in archive.
concerns: GB200 NVL72 and '35x lower cost' hardware claims not confirmed by OFox source in WebFetch — may appear in full article but could not be verified
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Approved. Comprehensive, well-balanced AI model news with strong benchmark coverage and honest competitive framing. Minor unconfirmed hardware claim, non-blocking.
